name: Potential HTTP DoS Flooding
notes: This rule triggers when a web server is flooded with a http request. This could indicate a malicious attacker initiating a series of http requests to a web server thereby flooding it with more http requests than it can process.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Web Servers
when the event context is Remote to Local
when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.Request Successful, Application.Client Connection Refused
when at least 50 events are seen with the same Source IP and different URL Host (custom) in 10 minutes
when the event matches "Response Code" MATCHES'(200|403)' AND LOWER("Method") MATCHES'(get|post)' AQL filter query
thresholds: when at least 50 events are seen with the same Source IP and different URL Host (custom) in 10 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Potential HTTP DoS Flooding on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Web Servers
AND when the event context is Remote to Local
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.Request Successful, Application.Client Connection Refused
AND when at least 50 events are seen with the same Source IP and different URL Host (custom) in 10 minutes
AND when the event matches "Response Code" MATCHES'(200|403)' AND LOWER("Method") MATCHES'(get|post)' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Potential HTTP DoS Flooding
Event description: Detects when a web server is flooded with a http request.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
